Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First given the process Create Purchase Requisition Approve Purchase Requisition Create Purchase Order Receive Goods Receive Invoice Match Invoice to PO Pay Invoice Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In the given underfitting model they have a parallel block of five activities create po receive goods receive invoice match invoice pay invoice This allows all sorts of illegal sequences for example pay invoice before receive goods or receive invoice match before both goods and invoice create po after receive goods

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In typical P2P sequence Create Req Approve Req Create PO then receive goods and receive invoice can be interleaved In practice after PO is created goods receipt and invoice receipt happen then match then payment then close

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After PO creation you can receive goods and invoice they can be in any order But you cannot match until both goods receipt and invoice have been received you need both to match

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: To fix we need to enforce that create po occurs after approve req then goods and invoice can be in any order parallel but must be before match then match then pay then close

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The only parallelism is between receiving goods and receiving invoice which is permissible no other activities can be swapped or omitted The model enforces ordering

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus the corrected process tree Sequence create req approve req create po Parallel receive goods receive invoice then match invoice then pay invoice then close case
